Container.Name: nebularr
Container.Overview:
Nebularr app (same role as [i]docker-compose.yml[/i] [i]app[/i] service): Web UI, API, metrics, webhooks.
[b]Requires[/b] [i]nebularr-postgres[/i] on the same custom Docker network [b]nebularr[/b] (create the network under Docker → Networks if needed).
[b]DATABASE_URL[/b] should use the Postgres superuser on first boot (same credentials as [i]POSTGRES_USER[/i] / [i]POSTGRES_PASSWORD[/i] on [i]nebularr-postgres[/i]). The setup wizard [b]Database[/b] step creates [i]arrapp[/i], reassigns ownership, and writes an encrypted [i]arrapp[/i] URL to [i]/app/data[/i]. Restart the app after that step so the process loads the saved URL (you may then clear [i]DATABASE_URL[/i] if you want it out of the template).
[b]Disk:[/b] Map [i]/app/data[/i] to a host folder (required for encrypted DB URL persistence). The optional [i]/var/lib/nebularr[/i] path is a reserved host folder; remove that mapping if unused.

Config.Name: Web UI / API port
Config.Description: HTTP port for Web UI, REST API, metrics, and webhooks.

Config.Name: Runtime data (DB URL)
Config.Description: Stores encrypted DATABASE_URL after Web UI database setup. Required for bootstrap flow.

Config.Name: App host folder (optional)
Config.Description: Reserved host path; primary state is in Postgres.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: First-boot superuser URL (must match nebularr-postgres credentials). Hostname must be the Postgres container name on network nebularr.
